ab: nasuljivat mun. tucu a vuaq uri kemacu ti sinsi tjanumun a sema qinaljan a kivadaq ta vavulungan.
ch: A:大家好，老師今天要帶大家去部落訪問耆老。
en: A: Hello everyone, today I’m taking you to the tribe to interview the elders.

ab: pai pariya! uri kiqaqivuan amen ta vavulungan tua pinayuanan a kai.
ch: B:好喔!我們要跟耆老說排灣語。
en: B: Okay! We have to speak Paiwanese to the seniors.

ab: palamu anga itjen a djemaljun?
en: A: Are we almost there?

ab: palamu anga djemaljun.
en: B:Almost there.

ab: vuvu, mangtjez amen a ljemizav tjanusun.
en: A: Grandpa, we’ve come to see you.

ab: na! manu macaqu mun a kemacalisianan a qivu ayau! qa! pai pariyangata!
ch: B:你們會說排灣語啊!真是太棒了!
en: B:You guys speak Paiwanese! That’s great!

ab: nasuljivat mun. tucu a vuaq uri kacuin mun ni sinsi a sema puvaquan a masengseng.
ch: A:大家好，老師今天要帶大家去小米田工作。
en: A:Hello everyone, today I will take you to work in the millet field.

ab: ui, sinsi.
en: B: Yes, sir.

ab: kelju samazau a matjatjiljitiljv, pa’enetju anga kemacu ta nuturivecan.
ch: A:大家過來排隊，記得帶好工具。
en: A: Come and line up, remember to bring your tools.

ab: ui sinsi, nia inalapanga a nia turivecan.
ch: B:是的，老師，我們都拿好工具了。
en: B: Yes, sir, we’ve got our tools.

ab: djemaljun anga itjen?
en: A: Are we there yet?

ab: ini anan cadja anan.
en: B: Not yet, it’s still far away.

ab: vaiku a pazaljum.
en: A: You go watering.

ab: ui, tiaken nay a puzaljum.
en: B: Okay, I’ll water it.

ab: sau alapu ta sizazazazaw.
en: A: You go get the bird catcher.

ab: ui, vaik aken a zemazaw.
en: B: Okay, I’ll go and catch the birds.

ab: nasuljivat mun. tucu uri vaik itjen a sema zua itua kakataliduan tua ’emuzi’uzip.
ch: A:大家好，我們今天要去動物園。
en: A: Hello everyone, we are going to the zoo today.

ab: aicu a pariya! aki anema uri tja pakazuanan a sema zua ayau?
ch: B:好棒喔!我們要怎麼去呢?
en: B: Awesome! How are we going to get there?

ab: tjevavav itjen ta basu a sema zua.
en: A: Let’s take the bus.

ab: matatiljitiljivu, uri padulu-i anga itjen a kiveli ta kipu.
ch: A:大家排好隊，我們準備買票。
en: A: Everyone line up, we are ready to buy tickets.

ab: ui.
en: B: Yes.

ab: a i tjaiqayav mareka qayaqayam, a i tjailikuz mareka ’atjuvi, talidu a kisumadju.
ch: A:前面是鳥類，後面是蛇類，大家自己參觀。
en: A: There are birds in the front and snakes in the back.

ab: sinsi marekut aken ta ’atjuvi.
en: B: I’m afraid of snakes.

ab: inika makuda, sau pacunu ta ’aya’ayam.
en: A: It doesn’t matter, then you go and watch the birds.

ab: nu djemaljun ta icizikang pangtjez-i anga ki’epuan.
ch: A:我們一個小時後回來集合。
en: A: We’ll be back in an hour.

ab: ui, maljimalji sinsi.
en: B: Yes, thank you.

ab: anema su pinacunan a vuaq?
en: A: What did you see today?

ab: napacun aken ta liyav a ’atjuvi, izua uta liyav a ’aya’ayam.
ch: B:我看到了好多蛇，還有好多鳥。
en: B: I saw a lot of snakes and a lot of birds.

ab: kirimu, uri vaik anga men.
en: A: Hurry up, we have to go.

ab: mayanan, maluljay aku kula.
en: B: Wait for me, my feet are tired.

ab: ari seljangi a vaik a ljemanguy?
en: A: Shall we go swimming together?

ab: nanguaq! ari!
en: B:Yes! Let’s go!

ab: tjuruvu a cawcau imaza a ljemanguljanguy.
en: A: There are a lot of people swimming here.

ab: vacalj a izua zaljum, tjemaza-i sakamaya a ljemanguy.
ch: B:那裡水很深，我們在這裡游泳就好。
en: B: The water is deep there, so we just swim here.
